executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours. The Qilin ransomware group is leading the assault with 20+ Canadian targets including the Eastern Townships School Board, while the Clop gang has claimed 14 victims and Play ransomware has hit 9 organizations. High-profile Canadian victims include Canada Goose (hit by both CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), National Money Mart (Everest - database leaked), Markham Stouffville Hospital (Anubis), and the Municipality of North Perth (WorldLeaks). This coordinated wave spans healthcare, education, financial services, manufacturing, and critical public sector organizations across multiple provinces.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20+ Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Kuzco Lighting, Service Star Freightways, and multiple professional services firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: Clop

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1003 (OS Credential Dumping)

items.name: Play

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1021 (Remote Services)

items.name: SafePay

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe ransomware attack with over 100 organizations publicly compromised in 48 hours across all sectors. Critical infrastructure impacts include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Thunder Bay Counselling, Bison Family Medical, Labtician Ophthalmics), education (Eastern Townships School Board, College Superieur De Montreal, Teccart), public sector (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose attacked by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross (Sinobi), Kids & Company childcare services (Sinobi). No incidents involving Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, or Shoppers Drug Mart identified.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak (Everest ransomware), Morison Insurance Brokers (Sinobi), and multiple accounting/financial services firms including Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin). No incidents involving major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) identified.

report_2w.executive_summary: The Citrix NetScaler vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) is being actively exploited in the wild and has triggered alerts from both CCCS and CISA. A massive supply chain attack compromised the Axios npm package—used 400 million times monthly—allowing attackers to inject malware into developer environments worldwide. Additionally, critical flaws in F5 BIG-IP (CVE-2025-53521, severity upgraded to 9.8) and Microsoft SharePoint Server (CVE-2026-20963) are under active exploitation. The Qilin ransomware group claims to have breached Dow Inc., and the TeamPCP threat actor has expanded operations from open-source supply chains into AWS cloud environments.

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.software_affected: Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.cia_impact: Unknown - CCCS advisory does not specify impact details

items.description: Critical vulnerability impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server
items.cia_impact: Unknown - CCCS advisory indicates critical severity

items.description: Critical F5 BIG-IP flaw upgraded to 9.8 severity allowing remote code execution
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Axios npm supply chain attack via compromised maintainer account
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.description: Previously disclosed Citrix NetScaler vulnerability referenced in context of CVE-2026-3055
CISA adds Citrix NetScaler to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog

items.name: Silver Fox
items.targets: Asia region organizations
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1219 (Remote Access Software) - Uses AtlasCross RAT and fake domains for credential harvesting

items.name: TeamPCP
items.targets: Supply chain targets including Databricks, AstraZeneca; expanded to AWS environments
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - Dual ransomware operations, exploiting stolen supply chain secrets

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Dow Inc. (chemical manufacturer), Corewell Health, Rocky Mountain Care
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - Double extortion ransomware

items.name: Iranian Threat Actors
items.targets: FBI Director's personal email
items.ttps: T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - Compromised personal email, exfiltrated CV and photos

items.name: Russian CTRL Toolkit Operators
items.targets: Enterprise networks via RDP hijacking
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol), T1090 (Proxy) - Malicious LNK files, FRP tunnels for RDP hijacking

items.name: DeepLoad Malware Operators
items.targets: Enterprise networks
items.ttps: T1204.001 (User Execution: Malicious Link), T1047 (Windows Management Instrumentation), T1555.003 (Credentials from Web Browsers) - ClickFix technique, WMI persistence, browser credential theft

items.name: EvilTokens
items.targets: Microsoft account users globally, including Canada
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Spearphishing Link), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie) - Phishing-as-a-Service platform for Microsoft account takeover

items.name: ResokerRAT Operators
items.targets: General enterprise targets
items.ttps: T1219 (Remote Access Software), T1113 (Screen Capture), T1547 (Boot or Logon Autostart Execution) - Telegram-based RAT with screenshot capture and persistence

items.name: Axios NPM Supply Chain Attackers
items.targets: Developers using Axios npm package (400 million monthly downloads)
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - Compromised maintainer account, postinstall RAT dropper

items.name: Genesis Ransomware
items.targets: Modern Advanced Print Solutions (MAPS, Inc.)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - Ransomware with data exfiltration

items.name: LeakNet
items.targets: Brute Wrestling
items.ttps: T1110 (Brute Force), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - Brute force attack leading to full compromise

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS issued two critical alerts in March 2026: CVE-2026-3055 affecting Citrix NetScaler (actively exploited) and CVE-2026-20963 affecting Microsoft SharePoint Server. The EvilTokens phishing-as-a-service platform is specifically targeting Microsoft accounts in Canada alongside global operations. Tax season phishing campaigns are actively targeting Canadian taxpayers using IRS and CRA lures.
